name: CHEMISTRY (Theory)
question_paper_html:
Compartment Exam - 2019

Code: 56/1/3 (set-3)

  • Please check that this question paper contains 15 printed pages.
  • Code number given on the right hand side of the question paper should be written on the title page of the answer-book by the candidate.
  • Please check that this question paper contains 27 questions.
  • Please write down the Serial Number of the question before attempting it
  • 15 minute time has been allotted to read this question paper. The question paper will be distributed at 10.15 a.m. From 10.15 a.m. to 10.30 a.m., the students will read the question paper only and will not write any answer on the answer-book during this period.

CHEMISTRY (Theory)

Time allowed:3 hours
Maximum Marks:70

General Instructions :

  1. All questions are compulsory.
  2. Section A: Questions number 1 to 5 are very short answer questions and carry 1 mark each.
  3. Section B: Questions number 6 to 12 are short answer questions and carry 2 marks each.
  4. Section C: Questions number 13 to 24 are also short answer questions and carry 3 marks each.
  5. Section D: Questions number 25 to 27 are long answer questions and carry 5 marks each.
  6. There is no overall choice. However, an internal choice has been provided in two questions of one mark, two questions of two marks, four questions of three marks and all the three questions of five marks weightage. You have to attempt only one of the choices in such questions.
  7. Use of log tables, if necessary. Use of calculators is not allowed.

SECTION A
1.

Identify the monomers of the following polymer:

 1
2.

What are the hydrolysis products of lactose?

 1
3.

Draw the structure of semicarbazone of cyclopentanone.

 1

OR

Draw the structure of product formed when propanal is treated with zinc amalgam and concentrated hydrochloric acid.

 1
4.

Predict the number of unpaired electrons in the square planar [Pt(CN)4]2-ion.

 1

OR

Amongst [Fe(C2O4)3]3- and [Fe(NH3)6]3+ which is more stable and why?

 1
5.

Name a substance that can be used as an antiseptic as well as disinfectant.

 1

SECTION B
6.
  1. Atoms of element Q form ccp lattice and those of the element P occupy {tex}\frac{2}{3}rd{/tex} of tetrahedral voids. What is the formula of the compound formed by the elements P and Q?
  2. What type of stoichiometric defect is shown by KCl and why?
 2
7.

Aluminium crystallises in a fcc structure. Atomic radius of the metal is 125 pm. What is the length of the side of unit cell of the metal?

 2

OR

The compound CuCl has fcc structure like ZnS. Its density is 3.04 g cm-3.
.What is the volume of unit cell?
Given: Atomic mass of Cu = 63.5 u; Cl = 35.5 u
NA = 6.02 {tex}\times{/tex} 1023 mol-1

 2
8.

Give reasons:

  1. MnO is basic whereas Mn2O7 is acidic in nature.
  2. Transition metals form alloys.
 2
9.
  1. Draw the structure of XeF4.
  2. What happens when CaF2 reacts with conc. H2SO4? Write balanced chemical equation.
 2
10.

Predict the major product obtained when t-butyl bromide reacts with sodium methoxide. Also, give its IUPAC name.

 2

OR
  1. Show the chemical reaction with bond movements and arrows for the nucleophilic attack of water on carbocation in acid catalysed hydration of alkenes.
  2. Give IUPAC name for the following:
 2
11.

For a reaction, C2H4 (g) + H2 (g) {tex}\to{/tex} C2H6 (g), rate = 5.5 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-14 [C2H4].

  1. Write the unit of rate constant.
  2. Calculate its half-life (t1/2).
 2
12.

Define conductivity and molar conductivity for the solution of an electrolyte. Why does the conductivity of solution decrease with dilution?

 2

SECTION C
13.
  1. What is the difference between calamine and malachite?
  2. Why is zinc used instead of Cu for recovery of Ag from [Ag(CN)2]-?
  3. What is the role of cryolite in metallurgy of Al?
 3

OR
  1. Give two points of differences between pig iron and cast iron.
  2. Outline the principle of zone refining.
 3
14.
  1. Draw the structures of geometrical isomers of [Fe(NH3)2 (CN)4]-.
  2. [NiCl4]2- is paramagnetic while [Ni(CO)4] is diamagnetic though both are tetrahedral. Why? [Atomic number of Ni = 28]
 3

OR

Define the following:

  1. Ambidentate ligands
  2. Spectro chemical series
  3. Heteroleptic complexes
 3
15.
  1. Write chemical equations involved in the preparation of KMnO4 from MnO2.
  2. Actinoids show wide range of oxidation states. Why?
 3
16.

Zinc rod is dipped in 0.01 M solution of zinc sulphate when temperature is 298 K. Calculate the electrode potential of zinc.
(Given: {tex} \mathrm{E}_{\mathrm{Zn}^{2+} / \mathrm{Zn}}^0 {/tex} = -0.76 V; log 10 = 1)

 3
17.

Answer the following :

  1. Why is ester hydrolysis slow in the beginning and then becomes faster after some time?
  2. In a solution of methylene blue, animal charcoal is added, the solution is then well shaken. What will be observed and why?
  3. Give an example of oil in water emulsion.
 3

OR

Define the following:

  1. Associated colloids
  2. Electrophoresis
  3. Zeta potential
 3
18.

The rate constant of a first order reaction increases from 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-2 to 6 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-2 when the temperature changes from 300 K to 320 K. Calculate the energy of activation.
(Given : log 2 = 0.3010, log 3 = 0.4771, log 4 = 0.6021)

 3
19.
  1. Differentiate between antiseptic and disinfectant. Give one example of each.
  2. Why do we require artificial sweetening agents?
 3

OR

Define the following terms:

  1. Tranquilizers
  2. Ant-acids
  3. Analgesics
 3
20.

Write the names and structures of the monomers of the following polymers:

  1. Teflon
  2. Terylene
  3. Buna-N
 3
21.
  1. What happens when D-glucose is treated with the following reagents:
    1. HI
    2. conc. HNO3
  2. What is the basic structural difference between starch and cellulose?
 3
22.
  1. Identify ‘A’ and ‘B’ in the following reaction:
  2. Why does aniline not undergo Friedel-Crafts reaction?
 3
23.
  1. How will you convert the following:
    1. Ethanal to propan-2-ol
    2. Phenol to 2-hydroxyacetophenone
  2. Why is phenol more acidic than ethanol?
 3
24.
  1. Write the product formed when
    1. 2-chloropropane is treated with alc. KOH.
    2. Aniline reacts with conc. H2SO4 at 453 - 473 K.
  2. When aniline is heated with CHCl3 and alc. KOH, a foul smelling compound is formed. What is this compound?
 3

SECTION D
25.

A crystalline solid ‘A’ burns in air to form a gas ‘B’ which turns lime water milky. The gas is also produced as a by-product during roasting of sulphide ore. This gas decolourises acidified KMnO4 (aq.) solution and reduces Fe3+ to Fe2+. Identify ‘A’ and ‘B’ and write the reactions involved.

 5

OR

Answer the following :

  1. Arrange the following hydrides of Group 16 elements in the decreasing order of their acidic strength: H2O, H2S, H2Se, H2Te
  2. Which one of {tex}\mathrm{PCI}_4^+{/tex} and {tex}\mathrm{PCI}_4^-{/tex} is not likely to exist and why?
  3. Which allotrope of sulphur is thermally stable at room temperature?
  4. Write the formula of a compound of phosphorus which is obtained when conc. HNO3 oxidises P4.
  5. Why does PCl3 fume in moisture?
 5
26.
  1. An organic compound with the molecular formula C7H6O forms 2,4-DNP derivative, reduces Tollen’s reagent and undergoes Cannizzaro reaction. On oxidation, it gives benzoic acid. Identify the compound and state the reactions involved.
  2. Give chemical tests to distinguish between the following pair of compounds:
    1. Phenol and propanol
    2. Benzoic acid and benzene
 5

OR
  1. Predict the products of the following:
    {tex}{CH}_3{CH}_2OH\;\xrightarrow[{H_2SO_4}]{K_2{Cr}_2O_7}\;A\;\xrightarrow{SOCI_2}B\;\xrightarrow{NH_3}\;C\xrightarrow{NaOBr}D{/tex}
  2. Arrange the following in increasing order of acidic character:
    HCOOH, CF3COOH, ClCH2COOH, CCl3COOH
 5
27.
  1. A 5% solution (by mass) of cane sugar in water has a freezing point of 271 K. Calculate the freezing point of 5% solution (by mass) of glucose in water. The freezing point of pure water is 273.15 K.
  2. Why is osmotic pressure of 1 M KCl higher than 1 M urea solution?
  3. What type of liquids form ideal solutions?
 5

OR
  1. 1.0 g of a non-electrolyte solute dissolved in 50 g of benzene lowered the freezing point of benzene by 0.40 K. The freezing point depression constant of benzene is 5.12 K kg mol-1, Find the molar mass of the solute.
  2. What is the significance of Henry’s law constant, KH?
  3. What leads to anoxia?
 5

answer_key_html:
Compartment Exam - 2019

Code: 56/1/3 (set-3)

  • Please check that this question paper contains 15 printed pages.
  • Code number given on the right hand side of the question paper should be written on the title page of the answer-book by the candidate.
  • Please check that this question paper contains 27 questions.
  • Please write down the Serial Number of the question before attempting it
  • 15 minute time has been allotted to read this question paper. The question paper will be distributed at 10.15 a.m. From 10.15 a.m. to 10.30 a.m., the students will read the question paper only and will not write any answer on the answer-book during this period.

CHEMISTRY (Theory)

Time allowed:3 hours
Maximum Marks:70

General Instructions :

  1. All questions are compulsory.
  2. Section A: Questions number 1 to 5 are very short answer questions and carry 1 mark each.
  3. Section B: Questions number 6 to 12 are short answer questions and carry 2 marks each.
  4. Section C: Questions number 13 to 24 are also short answer questions and carry 3 marks each.
  5. Section D: Questions number 25 to 27 are long answer questions and carry 5 marks each.
  6. There is no overall choice. However, an internal choice has been provided in two questions of one mark, two questions of two marks, four questions of three marks and all the three questions of five marks weightage. You have to attempt only one of the choices in such questions.
  7. Use of log tables, if necessary. Use of calculators is not allowed.

SECTION A
1.

Identify the monomers of the following polymer:

Solution:

Chapter Delete from the Textbook.

 1
2.

What are the hydrolysis products of lactose?

Solution:

Glucose + galactose

 1
3.

Draw the structure of semicarbazone of cyclopentanone.

Solution:

1

OR

Draw the structure of product formed when propanal is treated with zinc amalgam and concentrated hydrochloric acid.

Solution:

The carboxyl group of aldehydes and ketones is reduced to CH2 group on treatment with zinc-amalgam and concentrated hydrochloric acid (Clemmensen reduction)
{tex}\mathrm{H}_3 \mathrm{C}-\mathrm{CH}_2-\mathrm{CHO} \xrightarrow[\mathrm{HCl}]{\mathrm{Zn}-\mathrm{Hg}} \mathrm{CH}_3-\mathrm{CH}_2 \mathrm{CH}_3+\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}{/tex}

 1
4.

Predict the number of unpaired electrons in the square planar [Pt(CN)4]2-ion.

Solution:

Electronic configuration of Pt = 5d96s1

For square planar shape hybridization = dsp2
Hence, the unpaired electron in 5d pair up to make one d-orbital empty for dsp2 hybridization. Thus, there is no unpaired electron.

 1

OR

Amongst [Fe(C2O4)3]3- and [Fe(NH3)6]3+ which is more stable and why?

Solution:

[Fe(C2O4)3]3- ; {tex} \mathrm{C}_2 \mathrm{O}_4^{2-} {/tex} is a didentate / chelating ligand so it is more stable

 1
5.

Name a substance that can be used as an antiseptic as well as disinfectant.

Solution:

0.2% of phenol acts as antiseptic whereas 1% solution acts as disinfectant.

 1

SECTION B
6.
  1. Atoms of element Q form ccp lattice and those of the element P occupy {tex}\frac{2}{3}rd{/tex} of tetrahedral voids. What is the formula of the compound formed by the elements P and Q?
  2. What type of stoichiometric defect is shown by KCl and why?
Solution:

Chapter Delete from the Textbook.

 2
7.

Aluminium crystallises in a fcc structure. Atomic radius of the metal is 125 pm. What is the length of the side of unit cell of the metal?

Solution:

 Chapter Delete from the Textbook.

 2

OR

The compound CuCl has fcc structure like ZnS. Its density is 3.04 g cm-3.
.What is the volume of unit cell?
Given: Atomic mass of Cu = 63.5 u; Cl = 35.5 u
NA = 6.02 {tex}\times{/tex} 1023 mol-1

Solution:

Chapter Delete from the Textbook.

 2
8.

Give reasons:

  1. MnO is basic whereas Mn2O7 is acidic in nature.
  2. Transition metals form alloys.
Solution:
  1. Because Mn is in lower oxidation state of +2 in MnO whereas +7 in Mn2O7.
  2. Because of almost similar atomic radii.
 2
9.
  1. Draw the structure of XeF4.
  2. What happens when CaF2 reacts with conc. H2SO4? Write balanced chemical equation.
Solution:
  2. HF is formed , CaF2 + H2SO4 {tex}\to{/tex} 2HF + CaSO4
 2
10.

Predict the major product obtained when t-butyl bromide reacts with sodium methoxide. Also, give its IUPAC name.

Solution:

{tex}{\mathrm{CH}}_3-\underset{\underset{{\mathrm{CH}}_3}\vert}{\mathrm C}={\mathrm{CH}}_2{/tex} ; 2-Methylpropene

 2

OR
  1. Show the chemical reaction with bond movements and arrows for the nucleophilic attack of water on carbocation in acid catalysed hydration of alkenes.
  2. Give IUPAC name for the following:
Solution:
  2. 2,6-dimethylphenol
 2
11.

For a reaction, C2H4 (g) + H2 (g) {tex}\to{/tex} C2H6 (g), rate = 5.5 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-14 [C2H4].

  1. Write the unit of rate constant.
  2. Calculate its half-life (t1/2).
Solution:

Since its a first order reaction,

  1. Unit of rate constant is S-1 / time-1
  2. {tex} \mathbf{t}_{1 / 2}=\frac{0.693}{k} {/tex}
    {tex} =\frac{0.693}{5.5 \times 10^{-14}} {/tex}
    = 1.26 {tex}\times{/tex} 1013 S (or any other unit of time)
 2
12.

Define conductivity and molar conductivity for the solution of an electrolyte. Why does the conductivity of solution decrease with dilution?

Solution:
  1. Conductivity of a solution at any given concentration is the conductance of one unit volume of solution kept between two platinum electrodes with unit area of cross-section. Molar conductivity is the conductivity of solution for 1M solution.
  2. Because number of ions per unit volume that carry the current in a solution decreases.
 2

SECTION C
13.
  1. What is the difference between calamine and malachite?
  2. Why is zinc used instead of Cu for recovery of Ag from [Ag(CN)2]-?
  3. What is the role of cryolite in metallurgy of Al?
Solution:

Chapter Delete from the Textbook.

 3

OR
  1. Give two points of differences between pig iron and cast iron.
  2. Outline the principle of zone refining.
Solution:

Chapter Delete from the Textbook.

 3
14.
  1. Draw the structures of geometrical isomers of [Fe(NH3)2 (CN)4]-.
  2. [NiCl4]2- is paramagnetic while [Ni(CO)4] is diamagnetic though both are tetrahedral. Why? [Atomic number of Ni = 28]
Solution:
  2. In the presence of strong field ligand, CO, the unpaired d-electrons of Ni pair up so [Ni(CO)4] is diamagnetic but Cl- being a weak ligand is unable to pair up the unpaired electrons, so [Ni(Cl4)]2- is paramagnetic.
 3

OR

Define the following:

  1. Ambidentate ligands
  2. Spectro chemical series
  3. Heteroleptic complexes
Solution:
  1. Ligand that can ligate through two different atoms.
  2. Ligands can be arranged in a series in the order of increasing field strength. /An experimentally determined series based on absorption of light by complexes with different ligands.
  3. Complexes in which a metal is bound to more than one kind of ligands /donor groups.
 3
15.
  1. Write chemical equations involved in the preparation of KMnO4 from MnO2.
  2. Actinoids show wide range of oxidation states. Why?
Solution:
  1. {tex} 2 \mathrm{MnO}_2+4 \mathrm{KOH}+\mathrm{O}_2 \rightarrow 2 \mathrm{~K}_2 \mathrm{MnO}_4+2 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O} {/tex}
    {tex} 3 \mathrm{MnO}_4^{2-}+4 \mathrm{H}^{+} \rightarrow 2 \mathrm{MnO}_4^{-}+\mathrm{MnO}_2+2 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O} {/tex} (or any other correct equation of preparation)
  2. Because of comparable energies of 5f, 6d and 7s orbitals.
 3
16.

Zinc rod is dipped in 0.01 M solution of zinc sulphate when temperature is 298 K. Calculate the electrode potential of zinc.
(Given: {tex} \mathrm{E}_{\mathrm{Zn}^{2+} / \mathrm{Zn}}^0 {/tex} = -0.76 V; log 10 = 1)

Solution:

{tex} \mathrm{Zn}^{2+}+2 \mathrm{e} \rightarrow \mathrm{Zn}(\mathrm{s}) {/tex}
{tex} \mathrm{E}_{\mathrm{Zn} 2+/ \mathrm{Zn}}=\mathrm{E}_{\mathrm{Zn} 2+/ \mathrm{Zn}}^0-\frac{0.059}{2} \log \frac{1}{[\mathrm{Zn} 2+]} {/tex}
{tex} E_{Z_{n 2}+/ / 2 n}=-0.76-\frac{0.059}{2} \log \frac{1}{[0.01]} {/tex}
{tex} =-0.76-\frac{0.059}{2} \log 10^2 {/tex}
= -0.76 - 0.059 V
{tex} \mathrm{E}_{\mathrm{Zn} 2+/ \mathrm{Zn}} {/tex} = -0.819 V

 3
17.

Answer the following :

  1. Why is ester hydrolysis slow in the beginning and then becomes faster after some time?
  2. In a solution of methylene blue, animal charcoal is added, the solution is then well shaken. What will be observed and why?
  3. Give an example of oil in water emulsion.
Solution:

 Chapter Delete from the Textbook.

 3

OR

Define the following:

  1. Associated colloids
  2. Electrophoresis
  3. Zeta potential
Solution:

Chapter Delete from the Textbook.

 3
18.

The rate constant of a first order reaction increases from 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-2 to 6 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-2 when the temperature changes from 300 K to 320 K. Calculate the energy of activation.
(Given : log 2 = 0.3010, log 3 = 0.4771, log 4 = 0.6021)

Solution:

{tex} \log \frac{k 2}{k 1}=\frac{E a}{2.303 R}\left[\frac{1}{T 1}-\frac{1}{T 2}\right] {/tex}
{tex} \log \frac{6 \times 10^{-2}}{2 \times 10^{-2}} {/tex} = {tex} \frac{E a}{2.303 \times 8.314 \mathrm{JK}^{-1} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}}\left[\frac{1}{300}-\frac{1}{320}\right] \mathrm{K}^{-1} {/tex}
{tex} \log 3=\frac{E a}{19.15 \mathrm{~J} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}}\left[\frac{320-300}{300 \times 320}\right] {/tex}
{tex} 0.4771=\frac{E a}{19.15 \mathrm{~J} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}}\left[\frac{20}{300 \times 320}\right] {/tex}
Ea = 43855 J mol-1 or 43.855 kJ mol-1

 3
19.
  1. Differentiate between antiseptic and disinfectant. Give one example of each.
  2. Why do we require artificial sweetening agents?
Solution:

Chapter Delete from the Textbook.

 3

26.
  1. An organic compound with the molecular formula C7H6O forms 2,4-DNP derivative, reduces Tollen’s reagent and undergoes Cannizzaro reaction. On oxidation, it gives benzoic acid. Identify the compound and state the reactions involved.
  2. Give chemical tests to distinguish between the following pair of compounds:
    1. Phenol and propanol
    2. Benzoic acid and benzene
Solution:
  1. Compound = Benzaldehyde or C6H5CHO Reactions
    Reaction with 2,4-DNP

    With Tollens reagent
    {tex} \mathrm{RCHO}+2\left[\mathrm{Ag}\left(\mathrm{NH}_3\right)_2\right]^{+}+3 \overline{\mathrm{O}} \mathrm{H} \longrightarrow \mathrm{RCO} \overline{-}+2 \mathrm{Ag}+2 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}+4 \mathrm{NH}_3 {/tex}
    (Where R= -C6H5)
    Cannizzaro
    1. Add neutral FeCl3 to both the compounds, phenol will give violet colour.
    2. Add NaHCO3 to both the compounds, benzoic acid acid will give brisk effervescence of CO2

OR
  1. Predict the products of the following:
    {tex}{CH}_3{CH}_2OH\;\xrightarrow[{H_2SO_4}]{K_2{Cr}_2O_7}\;A\;\xrightarrow{SOCI_2}B\;\xrightarrow{NH_3}\;C\xrightarrow{NaOBr}D{/tex}
  2. Arrange the following in increasing order of acidic character:
    HCOOH, CF3COOH, ClCH2COOH, CCl3COOH
Solution:
  1. A = CH3COOH
    B = CH3COCI
    C = CH3CONH2
    D = CH3NH2
  2. HCOOH < ClCH2COOH < CCl3COOH < CF3COOH

27.
  1. A 5% solution (by mass) of cane sugar in water has a freezing point of 271 K. Calculate the freezing point of 5% solution (by mass) of glucose in water. The freezing point of pure water is 273.15 K.
  2. Why is osmotic pressure of 1 M KCl higher than 1 M urea solution?
  3. What type of liquids form ideal solutions?
Solution:
  1.  {tex}\Delta{/tex}Tf = Tfo - Tf = 273.15 - 271 K = 2.15 K
    {tex}\Delta{/tex}Tf = Kf m
    {tex}\Delta{/tex}Tf =  Kf {tex}\times{/tex} {tex} \frac{w_b \times 1000}{M_B \times w_A} {/tex}
    {tex} \mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{f}}=\frac{2.15 \mathrm{~K} \times 342 \mathrm{~g} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1} \times 95 \mathrm{~g}}{5 \mathrm{~g} \times 1000 \mathrm{~g} \mathrm{Kg}^{-1}} {/tex}
    = 13.97 K kg mol-1
    For 5% glucose in water,
    {tex}\Delta{/tex}Tf = Kf m
    {tex}\Delta{/tex}Tf = Kf {tex}\times{/tex} {tex} \frac{w_b \times 1000}{M_B \times w_A} {/tex}
    {tex} =\frac{13.97 \mathrm{~K} \mathrm{Kg} \mathrm{mol}^{-1} \times 5 \mathrm{~g} \times 1000 \mathrm{~g} \mathrm{~K}^{-1}}{180 \mathrm{~g} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1} \times 95 \mathrm{~g}} {/tex}
    = 4.08K
    Tf = Tfo - {tex}\Delta{/tex}Tf = 273.15 - 4.08 K = 269.07 K
  2. It is due to the fact that KCl dissociates to give K+ and Cl- ions whereas urea does not dissociate into ions
  3. Liquids having similar nature and polarities / which obey Raoults’ law
 5

OR
  1. 1.0 g of a non-electrolyte solute dissolved in 50 g of benzene lowered the freezing point of benzene by 0.40 K. The freezing point depression constant of benzene is 5.12 K kg mol-1, Find the molar mass of the solute.
  2. What is the significance of Henry’s law constant, KH?
  3. What leads to anoxia?
Solution:
  1. {tex}\Delta{/tex}Tf = Kf m
    {tex}\Delta{/tex}Tf = Kf {tex}\times{/tex} {tex} \frac{w_b \times 1000}{M_B \times w_A} {/tex}
    Mb = Kf {tex}\times{/tex} {tex} \frac{w_b \times 1000}{\Delta T_f \times w_A} {/tex}
    = 5.12 {tex}\times{/tex} {tex} \frac{1 \times 1000}{0.40 \times 50} {/tex}
    = 256 g mol-1
  2. Higher the value of KH, lower will be the solubility of a gas in the liquid.
  3. Low level of oxygen in the blood and tissues of people at high altitudes leads to the condition of anoxia.



 

 5
answer_key_text: Compartment Exam - 2019 Code: 56/1/3 (set-3) Please check that this question paper contains 15 printed pages. Code number given on the right hand side of the question paper should be written on the title page of the answer-book by the candidate. Please check that this question paper contains 27 questions. Please write down the Serial Number of the question before attempting it 15 minute time has been allotted to read this question paper. The question paper will be distributed at 10.15 a.m. From 10.15 a.m. to 10.30 a.m., the students will read the question paper only and will not write any answer on the answer-book during this period. CHEMISTRY (Theory) Time allowed:3 hours Maximum Marks:70 General Instructions : All questions are compulsory. Section A: Questions number 1 to 5 are very short answer questions and carry 1 mark each. Section B: Questions number 6 to 12 are short answer questions and carry 2 marks each. Section C: Questions number 13 to 24 are also short answer questions and carry 3 marks each. Section D: Questions number 25 to 27 are long answer questions and carry 5 marks each. There is no overall choice. However, an internal choice has been provided in two questions of one mark, two questions of two marks, four questions of three marks and all the three questions of five marks weightage. You have to attempt only one of the choices in such questions. Use of log tables, if necessary. Use of calculators is not allowed. SECTION A 1. Identify the monomers of the following polymer: Solution: Chapter Delete from the Textbook. 1 2. What are the hydrolysis products of lactose? Solution: Glucose + galactose 1 3. Draw the structure of semicarbazone of cyclopentanone. Solution: 1 OR Draw the structure of product formed when propanal is treated with zinc amalgam and concentrated hydrochloric acid. Solution: The carboxyl group of aldehydes and ketones is reduced to CH 2 group on treatment with zinc-amalgam and concentrated hydrochloric acid (Clemmensen reduction) {tex}\mathrm{H}_3 \mathrm{C}-\mathrm{CH}_2-\mathrm{CHO} \xrightarrow[\mathrm{HCl}]{\mathrm{Zn}-\mathrm{Hg}} \mathrm{CH}_3-\mathrm{CH}_2 \mathrm{CH}_3+\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}{/tex} 1 4. Predict the number of unpaired electrons in the square planar [Pt(CN) 4 ] 2- ion. Solution: Electronic configuration of Pt = 5d 9 6s 1 For square planar shape hybridization = dsp 2 Hence, the unpaired electron in 5d pair up to make one d-orbital empty for dsp 2 hybridization. Thus, there is no unpaired electron. 1 OR Amongst [Fe(C 2 O 4 ) 3 ] 3- and [Fe(NH 3 ) 6 ] 3+ which is more stable and why? Solution: [Fe(C 2 O 4 ) 3 ] 3- ; {tex} \mathrm{C}_2 \mathrm{O}_4^{2-} {/tex} is a didentate / chelating ligand so it is more stable 1 5. Name a substance that can be used as an antiseptic as well as disinfectant. Solution: 0.2% of phenol acts as antiseptic whereas 1% solution acts as disinfectant. 1 SECTION B 6. Atoms of element Q form ccp lattice and those of the element P occupy {tex}\frac{2}{3}rd{/tex} of tetrahedral voids. What is the formula of the compound formed by the elements P and Q? What type of stoichiometric defect is shown by KCl and why? Solution: Chapter Delete from the Textbook. 2 7. Aluminium crystallises in a fcc structure. Atomic radius of the metal is 125 pm. What is the length of the side of unit cell of the metal? Solution: Chapter Delete from the Textbook. 2 OR The compound CuCl has fcc structure like ZnS. Its density is 3.04 g cm -3 . .What is the volume of unit cell? Given: Atomic mass of Cu = 63.5 u; Cl = 35.5 u N A = 6.02 {tex}\times{/tex} 10 23 mol -1 Solution: Chapter Delete from the Textbook. 2 8. Give reasons: MnO is basic whereas Mn 2 O 7 is acidic in nature. Transition metals form alloys. Solution: Because Mn is in lower oxidation state of +2 in MnO whereas +7 in Mn 2 O 7 . Because of almost similar atomic radii. 2 9. Draw the structure of XeF 4 . What happens when CaF 2 reacts with conc. H 2 SO 4 ? Write balanced chemical equation. Solution: HF is formed , CaF 2 + H 2 SO 4 {tex}\to{/tex} 2HF + CaSO 4 2 10. Predict the major product obtained when t-butyl bromide reacts with sodium methoxide. Also, give its IUPAC name. Solution: {tex}{\mathrm{CH}}_3-\underset{\underset{{\mathrm{CH}}_3}\vert}{\mathrm C}={\mathrm{CH}}_2{/tex} ; 2-Methylpropene 2 OR Show the chemical reaction with bond movements and arrows for the nucleophilic attack of water on carbocation in acid catalysed hydration of alkenes. Give IUPAC name for the following: Solution: 2,6-dimethylphenol 2 11. For a reaction, C 2 H 4 (g) + H 2 (g) {tex}\to{/tex} C 2 H 6 (g), rate = 5.5 {tex}\times{/tex} 10 -14 [C 2 H 4 ]. Write the unit of rate constant. Calculate its half-life (t 1/2 ). Solution: Since its a first order reaction, Unit of rate constant is S -1 / time -1 {tex} \mathbf{t}_{1 / 2}=\frac{0.693}{k} {/tex} {tex} =\frac{0.693}{5.5 \times 10^{-14}} {/tex} = 1.26 {tex}\times{/tex} 10 13 S (or any other unit of time) 2 12. Define conductivity and molar conductivity for the solution of an electrolyte. Why does the conductivity of solution decrease with dilution? Solution: Conductivity of a solution at any given concentration is the conductance of one unit volume of solution kept between two platinum electrodes with unit area of cross-section. Molar conductivity is the conductivity of solution for 1M solution. Because number of ions per unit volume that carry the current in a solution decreases. 2 SECTION C 13. What is the difference between calamine and malachite? Why is zinc used instead of Cu for recovery of Ag from [Ag(CN) 2 ] - ? What is the role of cryolite in metallurgy of Al? Solution: Chapter Delete from the Textbook. 3 OR Give two points of differences between pig iron and cast iron. Outline the principle of zone refining. Solution: Chapter Delete from the Textbook. 3 14. Draw the structures of geometrical isomers of [Fe(NH 3 ) 2 (CN) 4 ] - . [NiCl 4 ] 2- is paramagnetic while [Ni(CO) 4 ] is diamagnetic though both are tetrahedral. Why? [Atomic number of Ni = 28] Solution: In the presence of strong field ligand, CO, the unpaired d-electrons of Ni pair up so [Ni(CO) 4 ] is diamagnetic but Cl - being a weak ligand is unable to pair up the unpaired electrons, so [Ni(Cl 4 )] 2- is paramagnetic. 3 OR Define the following: Ambidentate ligands Spectro chemical series Heteroleptic complexes Solution: Ligand that can ligate through two different atoms. Ligands can be arranged in a series in the order of increasing field strength. /An experimentally determined series based on absorption of light by complexes with different ligands. Complexes in which a metal is bound to more than one kind of ligands /donor groups. 3 15. Write chemical equations involved in the preparation of KMnO 4 from MnO 2 . Actinoids show wide range of oxidation states. Why? Solution: {tex} 2 \mathrm{MnO}_2+4 \mathrm{KOH}+\mathrm{O}_2 \rightarrow 2 \mathrm{~K}_2 \mathrm{MnO}_4+2 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O} {/tex} {tex} 3 \mathrm{MnO}_4^{2-}+4 \mathrm{H}^{+} \rightarrow 2 \mathrm{MnO}_4^{-}+\mathrm{MnO}_2+2 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O} {/tex} (or any other correct equation of preparation) Because of comparable energies of 5f, 6d and 7s orbitals. 3 16. Zinc rod is dipped in 0.01 M solution of zinc sulphate when temperature is 298 K. Calculate the electrode potential of zinc. (Given: {tex} \mathrm{E}_{\mathrm{Zn}^{2+} / \mathrm{Zn}}^0 {/tex} = -0.76 V; log 10 = 1) Solution: {tex} \mathrm{Zn}^{2+}+2 \mathrm{e} \rightarrow \mathrm{Zn}(\mathrm{s}) {/tex} {tex} \mathrm{E}_{\mathrm{Zn} 2+/ \mathrm{Zn}}=\mathrm{E}_{\mathrm{Zn} 2+/ \mathrm{Zn}}^0-\frac{0.059}{2} \log \frac{1}{[\mathrm{Zn} 2+]} {/tex} {tex} E_{Z_{n 2}+/ / 2 n}=-0.76-\frac{0.059}{2} \log \frac{1}{[0.01]} {/tex} {tex} =-0.76-\frac{0.059}{2} \log 10^2 {/tex} = -0.76 - 0.059 V {tex} \mathrm{E}_{\mathrm{Zn} 2+/ \mathrm{Zn}} {/tex} = -0.819 V 3 17.
